terms.definition: A problem-solving framework that solves overlapping sub-problems once and reuses their answers; requires optimal substructure.
Implementing DP as memoized recursion — write the recurrence naturally and cache results (e.g. with `functools.lru_cache`).
Implementing DP by filling a table from the smallest sub-problem upward in dependency order.
The minimal set of parameters that uniquely identifies a DP sub-problem; the key into the cache or table.
The equation describing how an optimal answer at one state combines optimal answers from smaller states.
The condition that the same sub-problem instance recurs many times in a natural recursion — the property DP exploits.
The property that an optimal solution to a problem can be composed from optimal solutions to its sub-problems.
Space-optimization technique: keep only the last few rows/columns of a DP table when the recurrence depends on a bounded window.
The DP recurrence at the heart of value iteration: `V(s) = max_a (R(s,a) + γ · Σ P(s'|s,a) · V(s'))`.
DP over a (time × state) lattice that finds the most likely sequence of hidden states in an HMM/CRF.
